event_title: Billy arrives and cuts his palm
core_objective: Billy's arrival at the isolated vacation lodge and introduce a blood-triggered supernatural anomaly when his cut palm bleeds and the blood vanishes on contact with the dirt.
canon_attributes: Location: an old vacation lodge in a tangled California forest | Characters present: Billy Cruz (29) arrives alone in a rental car | Persistent conditions: Billy wears a half-zipped hoodie over a worn unexplained YouTube t-shirt; long hair tied back under a black bandana; scruffy appearance, Billy’s blood drops from the cut; one drop hits the dirt and vanishes with no trace, Billy removes his black bandana and wraps his injured hand with it | Objects: A rusted gate blocks the gravel drive with a faded “NO TRESPASSING” sign on the iron bars, Billy yanks at the gate’s rusted lock and is sliced on a jagged edge, cutting his palm, A large black crow watches and tracks Billy with unblinking eyes | Other: Billy has a heavy gear bag and carries it as he starts walking toward the lodge
event_text_verbatim:
An old VACATION LODGE looms in the tangled California forest. Wind slices through overgrown brush. A rusted GATE blocks the gravel drive, a faded “NO TRESPASSING” sign flapping against iron bars.
A rental car grinds to a stop along the dirt shoulder.
BILLY CRUZ (29) climbs out—hoodie half-zipped over a worn paranormal YouTube tee, long hair tied back under a black bandana. Scruffy. Unimpressed.
He yanks at the gate’s lock—rusted shut. It doesn’t move. A jagged edge SLICES his palm. Blood drops. A single drop hits the dirt—and VANISHES. No trace. Billy rips the bandana from his head and wraps his hand.
BILLY
(grimacing)
Nothing says horror stream like tetanus.
He shoulders his heavy gear bag and starts the long walk. The LARGE BLACK CROW tracks him with unblinking eyes.

event_title: Billy enters and goes live
core_objective: Billy enters the boarded lodge, rigs it for a livestream, and initiates the live broadcast, ending with the first clear sign of an unnatural disturbance inside the house.
canon_attributes: Persistent conditions: Billy has a bloody wrap on his hand and tightens it before proceeding | Objects: Billy unlocks and enters through the boarded front door; the door creaks and closes behind him, Billy checks a sat phone showing one bar of signal, Billy texts Wesley ing the dead zone, that the house is rigged and live, and that the main gate is rusted shut, Billy has a pack, zips it, and heads toward the front door to leave | Environmental conditions: Billy stages the house with livestream scare/production equipment (trip scares, fog machines, lighting effects), A disturbance begins: lights flicker, temperature drops sharply, and a high-frequency whine emanates from the walls | Other: Billy uses a headlamp for illumination and turns it on inside the house, The interior air is cold and stale upon entry, Billy uses a rugged iPad with a jet-black touchscreen UI to control the setup and taps “go live”
event_text_verbatim:
Moments later, Billy unlocks the boarded front door. It CREAKS open. He pauses. Tightens the bloody wrap on his hand. Steps inside. A cold blast of stale air.
He flicks on his HEADLAMP. The door CREAKS closed behind him.
BILLY STAGES THE HOUSE FOR THE LIVESTREAM LATER THAT NIGHT – TRIP SCARES – FOG MACHINES – LIGHTING EFFECTS – THE WORKS.
He swipes through a jet-black touchscreen on a rugged Ipad UI: toggles, feed windows, diagnostics and taps the pulsing icon “go live”. The house comes to life.
Billy checks his SAT PHONE—one bar. Then texts Wesley.
BILLY
“Wes dead zone as advertised. House is rigged and live. Creepiest fuckin’ gig yet. Gonna scare the shit outta those kids. Main gate’s rusted shut. Enjoy the hike.”
He zips his pack and heads toward the front door. Then— lights flicker, a sharp DROP in temperature, as a high-frequency WHINE bleeds from the walls.

event_title: Scream, blackout, and something hits
core_objective: a sudden blackout coincides with an unseen hits near Billy, marked by Atramentus spreading and immediate violent sounds, then abrupt silence.
canon_attributes: Persistent conditions: Atramentus is explicitly named and framed as having tasted blood | Environmental conditions: A distant scream is heard from upstairs, A total blackout occurs; all power is dead, The sound of something heavy being dragged across the floor is heard, The moment ends in silence | Other: Billy is present and uses a headlamp, Billy raises the headlamp and sees a black ink stain spreading across the wallpaper like veins, Billy’s headlamp sputters out, A metallic snap is heard, A dry, ragged inhale is heard nearby, A visceral crunch is heard very close
event_text_verbatim:
DISTANT SCREAM upstairs. BLACKOUT. All power dead. Silence.
Billy slowly raises his headlamp. Across the wallpaper, a BLACK INK STAIN spreads outward like veins. Atramentus has tasted blood.
His headlamp sputters out. A metallic SNAP. A dry, ragged inhale. A visceral CRUNCH, too close. The sound of something heavy being dragged across the floor. Then silence

event_title: Wesley vlogs drive to Red Hallow
core_objective: Wesley and Kelly's arrival run-up to Red Hallow as a livestreamed “Session Zero,” while ing that Wesley has pre-rigged the experience via Billy and that Kelly is unsettled by the lack of signal and the staged setup.
canon_attributes: Characters present: Wesley Craven Wise (27) rides in the passenger seat (shotgun) and is being recorded by a mounted vlogging camera, Kelly Shear (23) is driving the SUV | Objects: Wesley addresses his audience on-camera, introducing Red Hallow and calling it “Session Zero,” and says they will play D&D in the condemned/cursed lodge on September thirteenth, Kelly has a glitching sat phone in her lap and reports navigation is dead, no signal, and even satellite is failing, Wesley says Billy described the place as the creepiest he has ever rigged, Kelly reacts with surprise on learning Billy rigs houses ahead of time, Wesley states Billy is his “secret sauce” and that the setup is turnkey, including cameras, fog, and jump scares | Environmental conditions: A sleek black Audi SUV drives down an overgrown dirt road in heavy mist/fog at night | Other: Wesley states that thirteen years ago the Miller family were butchered at the lodge while on vacation, with no suspects and no answers, Kelly shows unease and becomes quieter while looking at the dead satellite feed
event_text_verbatim:
Headlights slice through the mist as a sleek black AUDI SUV crawls down the overgrown dirt road.
WESLEY CRAVEN WISE (27) rides shotgun. Curated chaos and too-good lighting, every grin engineered for reaction clips. Tossed hair. Designer jacket. Minimalist tee that screams "casual, but rich."
A mounted vlogging cam records him. He flips on the influencer smile.
WESLEY
(to camera)
What’s up, weirdos. Tonight’s the one. Red Hallow.
Condemned. Cursed. Thirteen years ago, the Millers were butchered while on vacation in this lodge. No suspects. No answers. And tonight, September thirteenth, we’re bringing you something new. D&D in a fucking murder house. So hit that like button, drop your bets in chat, and let’s roll.
He swings the camera toward the foggy road.
WESLEY (V.O.)
Welcome to Red Hallow.
Welcome to Session Zero.
Driving, KELLY SHEAR (23). Layered thrift-store comfort and quiet defiance. Pale. Watchful. Glitching SAT PHONE in her lap. Her eyes stay on the screen—but track everything.
KELLY
Nav’s dead. No signal. Even satellite’s choking.
WESLEY
Billy said it’s the creepiest place he’s ever rigged.
KELLY
(surprised)
He… rigs the houses ahead of time?
Wesley grins at her reaction, a little smug.
WESLEY
Yeah. Guy’s my secret sauce. Whole setup’s turnkey. Cameras, fog, jump scares, you name it.
Kelly hides a flicker of unease. She didn’t know. She stares back at the dead SAT feed, quieter now.

event_title: Gate is open; they arrive at lodge
core_objective: Wesley and Kelly reach the lodge under overtly supernatural signs, including Kelly's ticking fractured pocket watch and the lodge opening its own front door.
canon_attributes: Objects: Fog is thick as their SUV reaches an iron gate that is now wide open, Kelly is holding an old fractured pocket watch that is ticking, The pocket watch shows 9:13, A black crow perches on the railing, watching, with red-glinting eyes, A shadow shifts behind a cracked second-floor window, watching, Wesley uses a flashlight under his chin and addresses a camera, The lodge’s front door groans open on its own | Environmental conditions: Billy’s rental car is present, abandoned in the fog, Wesley and Kelly arrive at a rotting three-story lodge with flickering veranda lights, They park and the cold air and mist cause their breath to fog the inside of the windshield | Other: Wesley notices something in Kelly’s hand, Wesley and Kelly each carry a gear bag as they exit the SUV
event_text_verbatim:
The fog thickens as the SUV reaches the iron gate—once rusted shut. Now, WIDE OPEN. They pass Billy’s rental car, abandoned in the fog. Oblivious. The lodge emerges. Three stories of skeletal rot, vines clinging like veins. Veranda lights choke and flicker.
Moments later, they park. Cold air presses in. Mist curls across the windshield, as their BREATH fog the inside glass. Wesley clocks something in Kelly’s hand.
WESLEY
You brought your spirit guide?
Kelly opens her palm—an OLD POCKET WATCH. Fractured. Ticking.
KELLY
He always follows.
TICK. TICK. 9:13.
He smirks, but it’s brittle.
WESLEY
Your ghost story, huh?
KELLY
I’ve told you what I can.
(beat, quiet)
Not everything.
They climb out of the SUV, each carrying a gear bag. A BLACK CROW perches on the railing. Still. Watching. Its eyes glint red.
Kelly pauses, looks up— A SHADOW shifts behind a cracked second, floor window. Still. Watching.
Wesley flips the flashlight beneath his chin, grinning for the camera.
WESLEY
(to camera)
Let’s go talk to some spirits.
The front door GROANS open—on its own.

event_title: Friends drive and talk about Lucy
core_objective: the group's bond and the emotional unease around Jaxton ignoring Lucy's calls on their anniversary as they drive toward the lodge.
canon_attributes: Characters present: Maverick Thomas (18) is driving; wearing a backwards cap, Holly Stanton (18) is in the front passenger seat; eyeliner and combat boots, Lucas Bosse (17) is in the back seat; hoodie up; fidgeting with his bag strap, Jaxton Willis (17) is in the back seat with a D&D binder in his lap | Objects: Jaxton’s phone receives multiple calls from Lucy; he silences/ignores them, Lucy is not physically present; she is contacting Jaxton by phone | Environmental conditions: Setting: Highway 56; dense fog; night drive implied by headlights | Other: Vehicle: an old Jeep, Jaxton and Lucy argued; Lucy is angry that he came tonight, It is Jaxton and Lucy’s anniversary, acknowledged by the group, The friends use sarcasm and D&D in-jokes to defuse unease, Lucas references previously 'disappearing' in sophomore year; the others 'waited' without pressing him, Lucas shares a brief, loaded smile with Maverick
event_text_verbatim:
Hours later. Highway 56. An OLD JEEP winds through dense fog, headlights brushing gnarled trees. Inside—warmth, friendship, nerves disguised as sarcasm.
MAVERICK THOMAS (18),backwards cap, drives. Swagger that fills the room and frays at the seams.
HOLLY STANTON (18), eyeliner and combat boots, leans into the passenger door. Warm. Eyes shadowed by something she can’t shake.
In the back, LUCAS BOSSE (17), hoodie high, fidgets with his bag strap. An artist’s stillness masking something unsettled.
-and JAXTON WILLIS (17), messy haired, D&D binder in his lap, stares ahead. A smile that hides something frayed at the edges.
His phone BUZZES: LUCY CALLING. He silences it. The others notice.
HOLLY
Not picking up?
JAXTON
We argued. She’s pissed I came here tonight.
LUCAS
Anniversary?
Jaxton nods once. The weight drops. They all know.
HOLLY
You don’t have to say it. We know what today is.
MAVERICK
(gentler now)
Lucy just wants you with her. Like she’s always had to be with you.
Jaxton says nothing. Stares ahead, guilt creeping in. The phone buzzes again. He kills the screen.
LUCAS
Ten bucks says this place reeks like mold and expired weed.
MAVERICK
Wrong. It’ll smell like Jaxton after a six-hour boss fight.

JAXTON
I showered. That’s emotional sweat you remember.
HOLLY
Because you traumatize us. Sentient pumpkins? Ooze orgies?
LUCAS
I still have nightmares about that cube.
JAXTON
You married it.
LUCAS
It was charming.
MAVERICK
Just your type.
They laugh. The tension eases, but the shadow of the day lingers.
HOLLY
I’m gonna miss this. Even your meta-gaming, Jaxton.
JAXTON
Settle for no mid-battle tears.
LUCAS
That’s a gamble.
(beat)
Sophomore year, when I kept disappearing. You all waited. No one asked. Just... waited.
HOLLY
You’re our bard, Lucas.
MAVERICK
Also the reason I drink hibiscus tea and know Bowie’s B-sides.
Lucas smiles at Maverick. Brief. Loaded.

event_title: They reach lodge and unload gear
core_objective: the group's arrival at the derelict Red Hollow lodge and their committed, upbeat resolve to begin their final “quest” there despite the unusual setting.
canon_attributes: Objects: They unload gaming gear, LED flashlights, and a 12-pack of beer, Holly has a flask | Environmental conditions: Red Hollow lodge is described as rotting/skeletal/crooked/massive with fog around the wheels | Other: Characters present: Jaxton, Maverick, Holly, Lucas, They arrive in a jeep that stops on gravel outside Red Hollow lodge, Maverick mentions beers and mushrooms as part of the night, Jaxton expresses guilt briefly, then forces a grin and frames the night as their last quest with “NAT 20s”, They move toward/onto the lodge steps and share a quiet smile before going in
event_text_verbatim:
They turn off the highway and crest the final rise. The lodge stands, rotting, skeletal, watching.
JAXTON
Can’t believe we actually have permission to stream here.
(beat)
We do have permission, right?
MAVERICK
Too late. I’m ready to punch a ghost.
HOLLY
This is for you, Jax. The best DM I’ve ever had.
LUCAS
Let’s make tonight count.
The JEEP rolls to a stop. Gravel crunches beneath the tires. Fog coils around the wheels like breath. RED HOLLOW, looms ahead, crooked, massive, waiting.
MAVERICK
(sarcastic)
Yup. Totally normal Friday night. Drive to a murder scene, unpack emotional trauma, roll initiative. At least we’re going out strong, beers and mushrooms.
HOLLY
(pulling a flask)
And if anyone grows a spine, a real drink.
They climb out of the jeep. Begin unloading: gaming gear, LED flashlights, a 12-pack of beer.
A beat. Shared silence. Jaxton looks at them, guilt flickering, then forces a grin.
JAXTON
If this is our last quest, we’re going out rolling NAT 20s.
Lucas grins, follows his friends up the steps.
LUCAS
This place has main character death energy.
JAXTON
It’s perfect.
They all share a quiet smile.

event_title: Maverick urges Holly to tell Jax
core_objective: Maverick presses Holly to admit her feelings to Jax before they go inside, ing her impending departure as the reason she must speak up now.
canon_attributes: Location: exterior veranda of Red Hallow Lodge, below the steps, same time | Other: Maverick and Holly are together and lingering below the steps, Maverick says he is "feeling off"; Holly jokes it is "the asbestos", Maverick urges Holly to tell Jax how she feels and states she has had a crush on him since day one, Holly states Jax is staying and she is not, Maverick says he is "full of secrets" but that tonight is not about him, Maverick and Holly walk up the steps and join Jaxton (Jax) and Lucas; the four stand together in a quiet moment
event_text_verbatim:
EXT. RED HALLOW LODGE – VERANDA – SAME TIME Maverick and Holly linger below the steps.
HOLLY You okay?
MAVERICK Just feeling off.
HOLLY
That’s just the asbestos.
She nudges him. No smile back.
MAVERICK (low)
Before we go in...
HOLLY Yeah?
MAVERICK
You need to tell Jax how you feel.
HOLLY (stiffens)
What?
MAVERICK
Don’t pretend. You’ve crushed on
him since day one. Fence climb check.
HOLLY
I fell three times.
MAVERICK
And he still made you the hero.
That’s love, right?
HOLLY
Doesn’t matter. He’s staying. I’m
A beat.
not.
Exactly why you have to tell him.
MAVERICK
14.
MAVERICK (CONT’D) You only get this kind of
connection once. She stares at him.
HOLLY What about you?
MAVERICK (small smile)
I’m full of secrets. But tonight’s not mine.
They head up the steps, joining Jaxton and Lucas. The four stand together. A quiet moment.

event_title: Crew enters and meets Wesley
core_objective: Wesley and Kelly already inside the house and frame the night as an unfiltered, real-time livestreamed D&D session in a “murder house,” introducing Kelly's role and unsettling appearance to the group.
canon_attributes: Location: INT. FOYER – REALITY - NIGHT inside a dark, damp house with mold/rot and a faint metallic smell; staircase visible | Objects: The group enters through the front door as it groans open, Wesley is already inside, adjusting a shoulder rig and checking angles, Kelly is with Wesley, calibrating microphones and adjusting a gain dial | Other: Jaxton recognizes Wesley (“Wesley Wise”); Wesley recognizes Jaxton as a top mod, Wesley states he is streaming everything in real time: D&D session, in a murder house, with no filters and no take-backs, Holly reacts to the house vibe (“Blair Witch”); Lucas questions the streaming/gear, Wesley introduces Kelly as on her first gig with him and labels her a ghost hunter for extra clicks, Kelly explicitly says: “I’m here to listen.”, The group exchanges awkward glances and is unsure who Kelly is until Wesley explains
event_text_verbatim:
INT. FOYER – REALITY - NIGHT
The front door GROANS open. The group steps into the dark.
Air thick. Damp. Heavy. Mold, rot, and something faintly metallic cling to the walls. The staircase climbing upward like a broken spine.
WESLEY is already inside, adjusting his shoulder rig, checking angles. KELLY moves methodically behind him, calibrating mics. She lingers on every shadow like it’s alive.
JAXTON (grinning)
Dude. Wesley Wise. In the flesh.
WESLEY
Top mod Jaxton, right? Appreciate
you pulling this crew together.
JAXTON
Appreciate the invite. This place
is insane.
LUCAS (eyeing the gear)
You’re really streaming this?
WESLEY
All of it. D&D. Real time. In a
murder house. No filters. No take- backs.
15.
HOLLY (sweeping her light)
You didn’t say it was full on Blair Witch.
JAXTON
You said you were bored.
HOLLY
Yeah. I pictured gym mats and bad
pizza. Not haunted insulation. Kelly adjusts a gain dial, monotone.
KELLY
It’s mostly structurally sound.
Awkward glances. The kids exchange looks, unsure who she is.
HOLLY
(to Wesley, nodding toward
Kelly) And she is...?
WESLEY
Kelly. First gig with me. Ghost
hunter. Extra clicks for the stream.
KELLY (flat)
I’m here to listen.
LUCAS
(to Jaxton, low)
She’s got the same vibe as the house.
JAXTON Not by acciden

event_title: They share mushroom chocolate bar
core_objective: The group collectively takes mushroom chocolate, re-ing their shared “toast” vibe while Kelly explicitly opts out.
canon_attributes: Other: Maverick produces a foil-wrapped mushroom chocolate bar, Maverick refers to it as a “Druidic offering” before a “toast”, Lucas frames it as like their old sessions and “nostalgic chaos”, Maverick and Lucas each take a piece of the bar, Holly offers a piece to Kelly, calling her “ghost whisperer”, Kelly shakes her head and declines, saying “No need”
event_text_verbatim:
Maverick pulls out a foil-wrapped mushroom chocolate bar.
MAVERICK
Druidic offering before the ritual?
LUCAS
Like the old sessions. Nostalgic
chaos.
They each take a piece.
16.
HOLLY (to Kelly)
You want one, ghost whisperer?
KELLY (shaking her head)
No need

event_title: Jaxton sets up and group explores
core_objective: while Jaxton sets up the game, Kelly detects the house as an active, listening appearance, shifting the exploration from casual banter into real supernatural unease.
canon_attributes: Objects: Jaxton is in the parlor unpacking terrain and dice to set up the game, Wesley, Holly, Maverick, Lucas, and Kelly are present in the house and begin exploring with flashlights, Kelly’s watch ticks once and the time shown is 9:13, The house is characterized as watching and exhaling (an implied sentient appearance within the scene) | Other: Kelly studies the wallpaper and observes a faint black stain that pulses outward, then vanishes, Kelly quietly states, "The house is already listening", The environment is described as cracked wallpaper and warped wood
event_text_verbatim:
Jaxton moves to the parlor, begins unpacking terrain and dice.
JAXTON
I’ll set up here. You guys should
explore.
WESLEY
Yeah, get the vibe. Ghost tour
before initiative.
HOLLY
Let me know when I need to seduce
something incorporeal.
MAVERICK
If I die before round one, I’m
haunting your dorm.
LUCAS (to Kelly)
Anything yet? Ectoplasmic mildew?
Kelly studies the wallpaper. A faint black stain pulses outward, Atramentus has tasted blood, then vanishes. Her watch ticks once. 9:13.
KELLY (quiet)
The house is already listening.
The others move, flashlights sweeping across cracked wallpaper, warped wood.
The house exhales. Watching. Wait

event_title: Tour montage triggers staged scares
core_objective: the haunted-house tour as a producer-rigged jump-scare bit while introducing the first concrete sign that something may be real: Maverick discovers a cracked sat phone smeared with blood.
canon_attributes: Persistent conditions: The sat phone is cracked and smeared with blood | Objects: Wesley states they rigged jump scares for the tour, Maverick opens a pantry door and finds rotting food arranged like a dinner party; a silver spoon glints in mold; he gags, Maverick finds a sat phone on the pantry floor, Maverick suspects the streams were faked based on the sat phone discovery | Other: Wesley narrates in voiceover as host of "Nerds in the Wild" (haunted edition), Holly opens a dusty music box in a child’s bedroom; cold air blasts from a hidden vent; she recoils and shrieks; she threatens to walk if hit again, Lucas examines oil paintings in a hallway; a distorted whisper plays from a speaker above
event_text_verbatim:
INT. VARIOUS ROOMS – REALITY - TOUR MONTAGE
WESLEY (V.O.)
Welcome to "Nerds in the Wild" —
haunted edition. Watch our brave party wander deeper into regret.
(beat) (MORE)
CUT TO:
17.
WESLEY (V.O.) (CONT'D) We rigged enough jump scares to
make them question every life choice.
– Holly opens a dusty music box in a child’s bedroom. The broken ballerina spins once, A BLAST OF COLD AIR shoots from a hidden vent. She recoils, shrieking.
HOLLY
Swear to god—next next one of these
hits me, I’m walking.
– Lucas traces oil paintings in the hallway. One shows a smiling family. As he brushes another, a DISTORTED WHISPER hisses from a speaker above.
LUCAS
Cool. Haunted house ASMR.
– Maverick opens a pantry door. Inside: rotting food arranged on overturned plates like a dinner party. A silver spoon glints in mold. He gags.
Nasty.
MAVERICK
He turns to leave, stops. On the floor, a SAT PHONE. Cracked. Blood smeared.
MAVERICK (CONT’D) (to himself)
Knew they faked their streams.

event_title: Sat phone found; blood shows real
core_objective: a hidden sat phone has been discovered near the pantry and that the blood used in the setup is shows to be real, undermining the assumption that everything is staged.
canon_attributes: Location: INT. PARLOR - REALITY | Persistent conditions: Kelly states the blood is real, Wesley attributes the blood effect to Billy being "a pro" and mentions Billy will check in tomorrow | Objects: Maverick finds a phone near the pantry and tosses it to Wesley, The phone is dead when Wesley tries to turn it on, Wesley hands the phone to Kelly | Other: Maverick, Wesley, and Kelly are present with the group regathered
event_text_verbatim:
INT. PARLOR - REALITY – MOMENTS LATER
The group regathers. Maverick tosses the phone to Wesley.
MAVERICK
This yours? Found it near the
pantry. Nice touch with the blood. Wesley, caught off-guard, covers with a grin.
WESLEY
Must’ve dropped it on walkthrough.
He tries to turn it on. Dead. Hands it to Kelly.
KELLY (low)
That’s blood. Real.
18.
WESLEY
Billy’s a pro. Said he’d check in
tomorrow. We’ll deal with it the

event_title: Polaroid states; Kelly warns it's real
core_objective: something in the location is not make-believe by ing Jaxton's hidden “REMEMBER” Polaroid and having Kelly explicitly warn the group that the place retains real trauma/regret.
canon_attributes: Objects: Jaxton flattens the game map on the table and handles his notes/notebook, A Polaroid slips from Jaxton’s notes showing “JAXTON + LUCY, age 10” at a flashlight campfire, Kelly is wiring microphones/mics and speaks calmly but coldly, Kelly states that not everything there is make-believe and that some places “remember” (regret/trauma/things that didn’t end right) | Environmental conditions: Wesley references a prior asylum shoot where gear glitched and shadows moved, and says they did not fake it | Other: Characters present: Lucas, Jaxton, Kelly, Wesley, Maverick, Holly, The Polaroid has “REMEMBER” scribbled on the white border, Jaxton quickly palms the Polaroid back into his notebook (attempting to hide it)
event_text_verbatim:
LUCAS
If this place had a therapist, they
fumbled their roll.
Jaxton flattens the game map on the table. His hands steady, too steady.
A POLAROID slides free from his notes: JAXTON + LUCY, age 10, flashlight campfire. On the white border, a scribble, “REMEMBER.”
He palms it back into the notebook.
JAXTON
Then it's working. The immersion.
Atmosphere. You're unsettled. You're inside the world now.
KELLY
(without looking up)
Not everything here is make- believe.
Everyone glances at her. She keeps wiring mics, calm but cold.
WESLEY (chiming in)
We did that asylum shoot last year, same vibe. Gear glitched, shadows moved, half the chat thought we faked it.
(beat) We didn't.
Maverick smiles, doubt on his face.
KELLY
You don’t need to live somewhere to
connect with what’s soaked in. (beat)
Some places remember better than people do.
HOLLY Remember what?
KELLY
Regret. Trauma. Things that never
ended right.

event_title: Wesley plays it off; chat reacts
core_objective: Wesley deflects concern by framing Kelly's behavior as an in-character gag while the livestream chat intensifies suspicion that something is wrong.
canon_attributes: Objects: Wesley addresses the camera and asks viewers to comment about Kelly being scary, Jaxton is present and spreads out dice and miniatures | Other: Maverick asks Wesley if Kelly’s behavior is “part of the bit”, Wesley refers to Kelly as already “NPC-ing the house”, Livestream chat visibly reacts with messages ing Kelly is possessed or that it isn’t scripted
event_text_verbatim:
MAVERICK (to Wesley)
Okay, is that like, part of the bit?
WESLEY
Roll her in, DM. She’s already NPC-
ing the house. (to camera)
Drop a comment if Kelly’s already the scariest thing in here.
LIVESTREAM CHAT LIGHTS UP:
**”KELLY IS POSSESSED???”**
**"she's not acting. i'm calling it."** **”this isn't scripted. no way.”** Jaxton spreads dice and minis.
JAXTON
She’s in. We roll in fi

event_title: Toast begins toast; sixth chair issue
core_objective: The group initiates their pre-game toast/toast and notices an unexplained sixth chair, while Jaxton alone receives a brief glitched-stream message that frames the coming story as a costly toast.
canon_attributes: Objects: Holly produces a silver flask from her bag, drinks from it, and passes it to Jaxton | Environmental conditions: Players seated at a table under a faint, buzzing chandelier light, From Jaxton’s POV, the stream glitches and briefly shows an old-school Game Master screen | Other: Group raises drinks and toasts: “To chaos. To summer. To stories that end better than they started.”, Kelly silently takes the fifth chair, There are six chairs present at the table; the group remarks that with Kelly playing there should be five, Lucas refuses to sit in the extra chair, A lower-third message appears to Jaxton only: “Start a story. states the wound. Pay the cost. Only then can you end it.” and vanishes before anyone else notices
event_text_verbatim:
The players settle into their seats. The table glows under the chandelier's faint, buzzing light. The mood shifts, nervous energy giving way to something looser.
Holly unzips her bag and pulls out a silver flask.
HOLLY
Don’t ask what’s in it. Just know
it'll make you braver.
She unscrews the cap and takes a sip. Passes it to Jaxton.
JAXTON To one last story.
MAVERICK
The official potion of courage.
LUCAS
Just mushrooms tonight. Back on
meds.
WESLEY (to camera)
For legal reasons, this is all soda.
Holly raises her flask. Everyone lifts their drink, cans, bottles, imaginary goblets.
20.
HOLLY
To chaos. To summer. To stories

The MAP ignites across the wall, burning letters into plaster: “THE WAITING ROOM.”
Lucy stumbles back.
Onscreen: her mouth moves, but the voice is not hers. The camera locks on her.
PARASITIC WHISPER (O.S.) It’s always recording.
Lucy's eyes brim with panic. She turns toward the dark, screaming.
LUCY
Jax, don’t let it take you too—
The SOUND CRACKS. Her body jerks, GONE.
INT. PARLOR – REALITY / ALTER REALITY – CONTINUOUS
Chaos detonates. Adrenaline becomes panic. Grief rips the air.
JAXTON (slamming door)
LUCY! LUCY!!
His voice cracks. He pounds until his knuckles split.
HOLLY (horrified)
She was right there, we have to get her out!
61.

event_title: Door won’t break and Kelly states past
event_text_verbatim:
MAVERICK swings a chair at the handle. Sparks. Wood splinters. No give.
MAVERICK
Come on! We can break it!
KELLY (snapping, trembling)
STOP! You’ll make it worse!
LUCAS (panicked)
Worse?! She’s trapped, Kelly! What the hell does worse mean?!
The TIMEPIECE in Kelly’s pocket TICKS. Rapid. dying heart.
JAXTON (voice shredding)
Lucy, listen to me! Don’t trust anything it shows you! Don’t trust it!
The walls PULSE. A LOW GROAN shudders through beast-like.
KELLY (hollow)
We’re not rescuing anyone. We’re trading turns.
A silence heavier than screams.
HOLLY (breaking)
What... do you mean?
Kelly’s hands shake. Tears burn as she forces
KELLY
I’ve been here before.
Everyone turns. Disbelief. Fear.
KELLY (CONT’D)
Thirteen years ago. My brother was
eight. We were on vacation, invited to a game night. Some older kids, my cousin’s friends. I didn’t even want to go. But I didn’t want to be the weird girl who stayed home.
She swallows hard. Her voice begins to break.
Uneven. Like a
the beams,
the words out.
62.
KELLY (CONT’D)
We played. Just like you. Just like
this. But the game didn’t end. The demon in the house wouldn’t let it.
Maverick stares, wide-eyed.
MAVERICK (stunned)
The Miller murders... that was you?
KELLY
They said I survived. That’s what
the papers wrote. But I didn’t. Not really.
(beat)
No one believed me. They blamed my cousin. Called it a psychotic break.
She looks at Jaxton. Then the sealed door.
KELLY (CONT’D)
The truth is... I let Puck save me. I ran. And the toll was my brother.
Her voice collapses. Tears streak.
KELLY (CONT’D)
I thought if I never told the story
again, it couldn’t find me.
The MAP BLEEDS. Dice twitch. The table shakes confession.
KELLY (CONT’D)
Then Wesley emailed. Said he had a
house for a stream. The address, I knew it. But I came anyway. To stop it.
HOLLY (furious)
Then why wait? Why not warn us?
KELLY (raw)
Would you have believed me? A stranger claiming she crawled out of an urban legend? I thought I could help without dragging you in. But the moment the map bled... I knew.
with her
63.
LUCAS (choked)
What does it want?
KELLY
To get out. And to gorge on every
fear it can before it does.

event_title: House crushes and Lucy is gone
event_text_verbatim:
The house SHUDDERS. Breath becomes frost. The ceiling bulges, lowering, as if the weight of the house is sinking onto them. BLACK WATER leaks from plaster, running down the wallpaper like veins.
HOLLY (whispered)
Is it... shrinking?
LUCAS (desperate)
No. No, it’s just in our heads. Has to be.
MAVERICK (gasping)
Then why does it feel like my ribs are caving in?
A MIRROR bleeds into existence above the fireplace. Slick, black glass.
Maverick stares. His reflection, older. Hollow-eyed. A wedding ring glints. Beside him, a blurred man’s hand rests on his shoulder.
MAVERICK (CONT’D) (hissing)
That’s not me.
KELLY Maverick—
MAVERICK (spiraling)
That’s NOT ME. NOT ME!
The reflection GRINS. The MIRROR SHATTERS outward. Glass explodes. Maverick drops, screaming, shards cutting his palms.
The FIREPLACE ERUPTS into blue flame. The parlor doors SLAM OPEN—
Lucy is gone.

event_title: Jaxton breaks down as map picks again
event_text_verbatim:
Jaxton collapses to his knees. His scream is raw, breaking.
JAXTON (shredded)
LUCY!!
HOLLY (devestated)
Oh my god... she’s gone.
Jaxton SLAMS his fists against the floor until blood streaks the boards.
JAXTON (sobbing)
No... no, she’s not! She’s not!
The MAP TWITCHES. A new vein spreads across parchment.
KELLY (hollow)
We lost her. (beat)
And now it’s picking again.
They turn. Maverick kneels in broken glass, shaking, the echo of his reflection’s grin still burning in his eyes.
The house waits

event_title: Lucy confronts the receptionist
event_text_verbatim:
INT. THE WAITING ROOM – ALTER REALITY
Lucy blinks. She’s no longer in the parlor.
Cheap vinyl chairs. A buzzing fluorescent light flickers above. A RECEPTION DESK. Empty.
Faceless figures sit frozen in the waiting area. Silent. Breathless.
A CLOCK on the wall TWITCHES instead of ticking.
LUCY (whispers)
Where am I...?
She rises, trembling, moving toward the desk.
A RECEPTIONIST appears. Back turned. Rigid. Hair taut. Suit too tight. Still as stone.
65.
LUCY (CONT’D) I need to leave.
(beat)
I don’t belong here.
The receptionist turns, no face. Just a BLACK, CRACKED MIRROR.
In one shard, JAXTON as a boy. Flashlight in hand, smiling. In another shard, LUCY’S OWN REFLECTION. Crying.
A CLIPBOARD slides toward her. The page blank. A PEN scratches on its own: “WHAT PART OF HIM DO YOU WANT TO KEEP?”
The pen begins sketching JAXTON’S SILHOUETTE before she even touches it.
Lucy staggers back, horrifie

event_title: House punishes the group and Jaxton
event_text_verbatim:
INT. PARLOR – REALITY – SAME TIME
CUT TO:
Maverick is dragged from the shattered mirror shards. Bleeding. Shaking.
The ceiling BUCKLES downward. The walls pulse inward, closer. The floor groans like it’s breathing.
KELLY (terrified)
It’s punishing him.
JAXTON (haunted, breaking)
No... it’s showing him.
The MAP curls inward, edges burning.
One word bleeds across in thick black ink: SACRIFICE. The D20 rolls itself. ONE.
JAXTON (CONT’D) (voice breaking)
A critical fail...
HOLLY
(pleading to the house)
Then take me! You hear me? Take ME! Silence. The flames gutter. The map burns again.
66.
A name bleeds across: JAXTON.
Beneath it: “THE STORY BELONGS TO ME NOW.”
The DICE TRAY SPLINTERS. The NARRATOR KING MINIATURE shatters into dust. From the base, a DARK HOLE opens.
LUCAS No. No no no no—
Jaxton steps forward, tears in his eyes. His body shaking like it’s no longer his.
JAXTON (soft)
It was never mine to control... (beat)
But it’s mine now to pay for. HOLLY lunges for him.
HOLLY
No! Don’t you dare! We need you,
Jax! I need you—
Jaxton cups her face. His hand trembles, but his voice steadies.
JAXTON
You gave me family when I had
nothing. You kept me here when I wanted to disappear. I’ll never forget you, Holly. Ever. I love you now, I’ve loved you forever.
He kisses her forehead. Holly collapses into sobs. Jaxton forces a smile through his tears.
JAXTON (CONT’D) (quiet)
I love you guys.
The HOUSE GROANS. Dice scatter across the table. Every single die rolls a ONE.
The MAP flashes: CRITICAL FAILURE. NO ESCAPE.

event_title: Lucy pleads through the mirror
event_text_verbatim:
INT. THE WAITING ROOM – ALTER REALITY - SAME TIME Lucy pounds the mirror-faced receptionist’s desk.
CUT TO:
67.
LUCY (screaming)
Jax! Jax, don’t you dare leave me again!
On the cracked mirror, JAXTON’S FACE PRESSES against the glass from the other side, suffocating, blurred in smoke.
Lucy SLAMS the glass, sobbing.
LUCY (CONT’D) (crying out)
Forever twins, remember? You promised! You promised, Jax!
INT. PARLOR – REALITY / ALTER REALITY
Jaxton glances upward, as if hearing her through the walls.
JAXTON (soft)
I hear you, Luce. Always.
He steps toward the smoke. The void PULLS him, but it does not close. His chair remains, empty. A hollow absence

event_title: Lucy opens the next door
event_text_verbatim:
INT. THE WAITING ROOM – ALTER REALITY Lucy SCREAMS, clawing at the glass.
LUCY (devastated)
I told you I’d protect you! I told you—
The receptionist turns. Mirror face etched with a single word: CONTINUE.
The clipboard writes: “THE NEXT DOOR IS OPEN.”
Lucy shakes, breaking. She grips the door handle, carved with a SPIRAL around an EYE.
She sobs, then turns it.

event_title: Kelly offers herself and disappears
event_text_verbatim:
INT. PARLOR – REALITY – SAME TIME
The group collapses in shock. Holly sobs uncontrollably, clutching Jaxton’s empty chair. Maverick smashes his fist into the floor, bloodied. Lucas can’t breathe.
Kelly clutches the TIMEPIECE like it’s burning her hand.
KELLY (whispers) Puck... help us.
Nothing.
Then—MAVERICK CONVULSES. His throat bulges. Black mist seeps out.
Holly and Lucas panic.
HOLLY MAV! What’s wrong?!
MAVERICK (choking)
It’s inside—
He sees his SHADOW SPLIT. Another him. Grinning.
MAVERICK (CONT’D) Get it out of me!
Lucas grabs him.
LUCAS Hold on, Mav!
Kelly steps forward, tears streaming, clutching the TIMEPIECE.
KELLY (to herself)
It’s me. It’s always been me.
She kneels, pressing the TIMEPIECE to Maverick’s chest.
KELLY (CONT’D) You want a toll?
(beat)
Fine. I’m offering it.
MAVERICK convulses harder. TICKING RETURNS. FAST. LOUD.
69.
MAVERICK
What are you doing—KELLY, STOP—
Don’t trade for me—DON’T—
A cold wind rips through the parlor. Lights EXPLODE.
HOLLY (screaming)
KELLY!
Kelly’s hair lifts. Eyes shimmer silver.
KELLY (whispers)
Protect them.
From behind, PUCK MANIFESTS. A ghost half-formed. Eyes like shattered mirrors.
PUCK (inhuman)
The balance is kept. He touches her shoulder.
COMPLETE DARKNESS.
When LIGHT RETURNS. KELLY IS GONE.
Only the TIMEPIECE remains. Ticking beside a BLACK SCORCH MARK.
HOLLY (sobbing)
No—no no no—KELLY!
She collapses. Her sobs shatter the others. Lucas shakes, horrified.
MAVERICK (whispers, broken)
She saved me. TICK. TICK. 9:13. GLOWING.
The timepiece SLIDES toward HOLLY. She backs away.
HOLLY (crying)
No, please. Don’t pick me—
It glows brighter. An unnatural, searing BLU

event_title: Older voice taunts as map changes
event_text_verbatim:
70.
HOLLY (CONT’D) (broken)
I don’t want to die.
The TICKING grows LOUDER. Like a countdown.
Suddenly— A LAUGH. Low. Layered. Ancient. Vibrates through the walls like a war drum. NOT PUCK. Something OLDER. HUNGRIER.
PARASITIC WHISPER (O.S.) It can’t protect you.
The MAP IGNITES. A new section scorches into the table: THE PROJECTION ROOM.
CUT TO:

event_title: Lucy is forced to watch reels
event_text_verbatim:
INT. THE PROJECTION ROOM – ALTER REALITY
A claustrophobic chamber. Walls the color of nicotine. The BUZZ of a dying ballast. Dust spirals inside a thin cone of projector light.
Six metal CHAIRS face a stained SCREEN. No one sits in them. Yet.
Behind Lucy, ancient 16mm REELS HUM. Tape slaps. A stutter that’s almost a heartbeat.
ONSCREEN: LITTLE LUCY (5) screams into a pillow, shoulders shaking.
LUCY
No.
The REEL swaps by itself.
ONSCREEN: TEEN LUCY argues with her PARENTS. Storms out. The door hits the frame. JAXTON lingers on the stairs, a D20 in his hand. Tears streak his face.
PARASITIC VOICE (O.S.)
Watch.
Lucy inches closer, breath fogging in the projector’s beam.
ONSCREEN: BASEMENT. Jaxton older holds that same D20 like a relic.
JAXTON (O.S.)
Lucy said she’d play. She promised.
71.
Lucy’s hand clamps her mouth. Cut. Flicker. Jam.
ONSCREEN: LUCY in a hallway. She glances back. Keeps walking. Behind her, Jaxton’s silhouette shrinks.
JAXTON (O.S.) (CONT’D) You never kept your promise!
The AUDIO WARPS, as if drowning. The FRAME BUBBLES— BLACK.
Then, JAXTON’S FACE APPEARS. Not film. Real. Too close. The light catches wet eyes.
JAXTON (O.S.) (CONT’D) You left me in the dark. You lied.
The celluloid IGNITES. Flame eats him to

event_title: Jaxton faces cards in the void
event_text_verbatim:
INT. THE VOID – ALTER REALITY
CUT TO:
Infinite dark. A sliver of stage light drops from nowhere.
A TABLE. A DM SCREEN.
Jaxton sits behind it, alone. Cards spread like a ritual circle.
His hand shakes as he flips the first: LUCY’S PHOTO (10) taped to cardstock. THE CARD: “THE RETURN.”
PARASITIC VOICE (O.S.) She’s watching now. But so am I.
The VOID SHUDDERS. Five more CARDS slide into place. One face- down card pulses faintly: “THE END.”
PARASITIC VOICE (O.S.) (CONT’D) Finish the story.
Jaxton swallows. Doesn’t flip

event_title: Lucy names betrayal and bleeding
event_text_verbatim:
INT. THE PROJECTION ROOM – ALTER REALITY
The SCREEN is a dead square. The PROJECTOR HUMS.
CUT TO:
72.
A TABLE SLAMS down in front of Lucy. Dust blooms like breath.
Six chairs. Burned paper. DICE etched with hairline cracks. Bone carved MINIS that don’t quite hold still.
PARASITIC VOICE (O.S.) You’ve always watched. Now... they’re
watching you.
The seats FILL with SHADOWS, featureless but familiar in posture. They all TURN to Lucy.
SHADOWS (IN LUCY’S VOICE) Say the words, Lucy. Say it so it’s
real.
The burned paper quivers.
New ink crawls across like veins:
“ONE MUST BETRAY. ONE MUST BLEED.”
“SPEAK. OR THEY ALL FALL.”
Lucy’s fingers dig into the table edge until her knuckles go bloodless.
LUCY (shaking)
I’m not part of this. I’m not—
PARASITIC VOICE (O.S.) Speak the choice. Name the traitor.
Name the blame. The SCREEN FLARES to life.
MIDDLE SCHOOL: MAVERICK shoulder checks Jaxton, grinning for a crowd.
A DARK CORNER: HOLLY kisses Jaxton, then pulls away like she burned herself.
A STAIRWELL: LUCAS, whispering to Holly, “Jaxton doesn’t care.” He does.
Lucy staggers, fury and shame warring behind her eyes.
LUCY Stop. Stop—
Say it.
PARASITIC VOICE (O.S.)
73.
The SHADOWS LEAN IN. The projector BUZZ drills her skull.
LUCY (breaking open)
Holly betrays him. Maverick bleeds. A jagged CRACK fractures the screen.
WHITE detonation

event_title: Lucy wakes; accusations and effects begin
event_text_verbatim:
INT. PARLOR – REALITY – SAME TIME
SMASH CUT TO:
Lucy GASPING awake, face down on warped hardwood. The room has SHRUNK, ceiling sagging low, BLACK WATER threading down like veins, breathing with the house.
HOLLY, LUCAS, and MAVERICK stand over her.
HOLLY (numb)
We heard you.
MAVERICK (quiet, dazed)
What did you do?
The TIMEPIECE lies beside Lucy, TICKING IN REVERSE. 9:13... 9:12... 9:11... then slams back to 9:13.
LUCY (choking)
It made me choose, I didn’t—
LUCAS You chose us.
The tablet flickers to a live feed that isn’t theirs. ON-SCREEN COMMENTS CASCADE:
**“get ready to watch him bleed.”**
**“roll again.”**
**“traitor marked.”**
Lucy crawls back, wrecked.
HOLLY (voice shaking)
Say it again. What you said.
74.
LUCY (pleading)
It wasn’t real— the room — it put the words—
MAVERICK (anger cracking)
You picked me to bleed.
LUCY (raw)
I didn’t want to!
HOLLY (disbelief)
You looked at me when you said “betray.” You didn’t blink.
LUCAS Guys—
The TIMEPIECE SPINS. Faster. Louder. The DICE lift and HOVER.
LUCAS (CONT’D) It’s doing it again—
Maverick’s nose starts to BLEED. Then his ears. He looks at his palm as a long, ragged TEAR opens across the lifeline, bleeding like a mouth.
MAVERICK (scream)
AHHHH—
He staggers backward, leaving a red trail.

event_title: Maverick bleeds and enters mirror room
event_text_verbatim:
INT. HALLWAY – REALITY – CONTINUOUS
Maverick stumbles through the corridor, hand clamped, bleeding, disoriented. The HOUSE seems to lean him toward a specific door.
He SLAMS through it—
INT. MIRROR ROOM – ALTER REALITY – CONTINUOUS
Bare. Dim. No windows. No furniture. Just the bulb’s FLICKER and an ANTIQUE MIRROR leaning on the far wall.
Maverick steadies himself against it, breathing hard.
75.
MAVERICK (to himself)
Get it together, Mav. Come on— He looks up.
JAXTON stares back from the mirror with black, bottomless eyes. Smiling wrong.
MIRROR JAXTON (almost gentle)
You know why she picked you. Maverick spins. Empty room.
Back to the mirror, only his reflection now... but his REFLECTION’S EYES BLEED.
Behind him (mirror-only), SCHOOL PHOTOS materialize and hang in the air like evidence:
- Jaxton alone at lunch. — Holly’s secret kiss.
— Lucas scrolling a chat where Maverick’s texts are screenshotted and laughed at.
— Maverick’s unsent draft to Lucas: “I think it’s you. It’s always been you.”
MAVERICK (terrified)
Stop. Please—
The bulb stutters. The ROOM BREATHES.
The photos PEEL AWAY. The mirror goes OILY BLACK. JAXTON APPEARS AGAIN — wronger. Smile cutting too wide.
MIRROR JAXTON You can leave. Just pick.
MAVERICK (cracking)
Pick...?
MIRROR JAXTON Who dies next.
A slick SILVER DOOR rises open in the wall. It reflects nothing.
76.
No.
MAVERICK
MIRROR JAXTON
One word. That’s all. Say the name.
The mirror flashes a fusillade: LUCY’S blood-slick hands... HOLLY burying her face after the kiss... LUCAS’s lie... JAXTON’S empty chair.
MAVERICK (sobbing)
Stop—
MIRROR JAXTON (soft, relentless)
SAY IT.
Maverick drops to his knees. Fingers in his hair, nails tearing scalp. Blood patters the floor.
MAVERICK
(a ruined whisper)
...Holly.
The SILVER DOOR CREAKS open.
Maverick staggers toward it, freezes.
IN THE MIRROR, HOLLY convulses, eyes rolling, a line of bright red threading down her lip.
BLACK.
MAVERICK (CONT’D) (shocked)
No... I didn’t mean—
MIRROR JAXTON You didn’t choose.
(beat)
You confessed.

event_title: Return to parlor around the table
event_text_verbatim:
INT. PARLOR – SAME TIME
The group surrounds the table.
Lucy still shaking, her hands stained red. Lucas watches the dice trembling in the tray.
77.

event_title: Holly convulses and dies
event_text_verbatim:
LUCAS (low, unsettled)
Something’s wrong.
HOLLY (urgent, breaking)
Where is he?
Suddenly—HOLLY GASPS. Her eyes widen. She clutches her chest.
HOLLY (CONT’D) I— I can’t—
She collapses to her knees. Chokes. BLOOD spills from her mouth.
LUCY (screaming)
HOLLY!
Her fingers twist unnaturally, joints locking backwards. Blood streams from her nose, her ears.
Lucas drops beside her, frantic.
LUCAS
She’s seizing—HELP HER!
The dice RATTLE on the table. The MAP bleeds VEINS of BLACK INK.
LUCY (pleading)
Please, please make it stop!
Holly’s body jolts. Once. Twice. Then... still.
Her chest deflates. A final shudder.
A single drop of blood lands on the TIMEPIECE.
It glows. Then fades.
Silence.
HOLLY lies motionless. Eyes open. Staring.
Lucy kneels beside her. Shaking. Hands soaked in red.

event_title: Maverick arrives; Lucas erupts
event_text_verbatim:
FOOTSTEPS. Maverick enters from the hall, stopping cold when he sees Holly’s body.
78.
MAVERICK (shattered)
...No.
Lucas cradles Holly, rocking her like she might wake.
LUCAS (raw)
She’s dead.
Maverick freezes, horror spreading across his face.
MAVERICK (voice cracking)
I didn’t—
Lucas rises slowly. FIRE in his eyes.
LUCAS (snarling)
But you said it.
Lucy stumbles between them, hands out.
LUCY (desperate)
He didn’t mean to. Lucas, it made him—
LUCAS
It’s always making us.
He SCREAMS. Kicks the table. Miniatures FLY, as dice scatter like teeth across the floor.
The MAP PULSES. A deep HEARTBEAT in the wood.
The livestream glitches. Faces in the chat warp into static mouths.
MAVERICK (hollow, broken)
It’s not asking anymore. WHISPERS crawl through the room.
PARASITIC WHISPERS (O.S.) Lucy. Maverick. Lucas.
Maverick steps forward, trembling.
MAVERICK (to the house) Take m

event_title: Timepiece reverses; Lucy states entry
event_text_verbatim:
The TIMEPIECE TICKS, backwards. Faster. Louder.
LUCAS (spitting)
It’s her. It picked her. Everyone stares at Lucy.
LUCY (quiet, haunted)
Maybe it let me in because it already knew.
MAVERICK Let you in?
LUCY
(a whisper, breaking)
No one asked how I got inside. (beat)
I didn’t break in. The window opened for me. Like it was waiting.
A silence heavier than air itself.
LUCAS (realization)
What if this whole thing... was meant for you?
TICK. TICK. 9:13. The sound echoes like a funeral bell.

event_title: Rules card rises and crumbles
event_text_verbatim:
INT. PARLOR – REALITY – MOMENTS AFTER HOLLY’S DEATH The room is paralyzed.
Lucy wipes Holly’s blood from her hands.
Maverick can’t meet Lucas’s eyes.
The map pulses faintly, flickering like skin stretched too thin.
On the table, the TIMEPIECE glows.
A CARD slowly RISES from the map.
Black ink across its face: “THE RULES.”
The lights dim. The house RUMBLES, like it’s clearing its throat.
80.
LUCY (reading it, soft)
“Start a story. Reveal the wound. Pay the cost. Only then can you end it.”
The card CRUMBLES to ash in her hands. They stare at the soot drifting down.
LUCY (CONT’D) (whispers, horrified
clarity)
That’s what it’s doing.
(beat)
It’s not just killing us. It’s... playing through us.
LUCAS (rage boiling)
Bullshit. That’s not a rule. That’s a curse.
Maverick swallows. Silent. His eyes wet.
MAVERICK (almost to himself)
Maybe it’s both.
LUCY (remembering, breaking)
Jaxton said... it only moves if we give it something true.
Silence. The weight of it pins them all. The TIMEPIECE ticks once. TICK.
The stream overlay glitches. For a single frame: “START A STORY. REVEAL THE WOUND. PAY THE COST. ONLY THEN CAN YOU END IT.”
Then gone.
The table bleeds. Dice split. A single word glows in red: WITNESS.
The group is frozen.
Then, a faint MECHANICAL WHIRR.
LUCAS What is that...

event_title: VHS appears; past game night deaths
event_text_verbatim:
LUCY (quietly) That sound...
They follow it.
INT. DEN – REALITY - MOMENTS LATER
An OLD CRT TELEVISION sits in the corner. Dusty. Dead for decades.
Beneath it, a VHS PLAYER whirs. The machine EJECTS a tape. Label: “MILLER GAME NIGHT.” Maverick stares, paling.
MAVERICK (whispers)
That... wasn’t here before.
Lucy, shaking, takes the tape. Slides it back in. Presses PLAY.
The screen hisses with STATIC.
INT. RED HALLOW LODGE – VHS FOOTAGE – NIGHT (1990)
A grainy, flickering video. Children crowd a living room. Pizza boxes, birthday balloons, a D&D board spread across the carpet.
They laugh. Roll dice.
One boy in a wizard hat, JAMES MILLER (14), leans forward.
JAMES
(ghostly through static)
Okay, you found the locked door. It whispers to you...
Behind him, THE LIGHTS FLICKER.
The camera jitters. Another teen SCREAMS.
The room TILTS sideways. The lens distorts.
One kid convulses violently. Another gasps for air. PANIC erupts. Parents rush in—
82.
The feed distorts, faces stretched, voices warped.
CUT TO BLACK.
INT. DEN – REALITY - PRESENT - SAME TIME The group stands frozen.
The screen glows with STATIC.
A faint RED LIGHT blinks on the VCR: *REC.*
MAVERICK (staggered, horrified)
They died here...
LUCAS
And it filmed itself.
They stand in silence.
The whirring tape recorder seems to breathe. The house itself is

event_title: Jaxton chooses and is consumed
event_text_verbatim:
INT. VOID ROOM – ALTERED REALITY
A table. Darkness pressing from every angle.
A single SPOTLIGHT isolates JAXTON. He looks hollowed out, barely standing.
Scattered before him: burned notes, torn maps, the melted remains of miniatures.
One CARD lies facedown. It PULSES, faint light bleeding beneath it.
Letters etch across its back: “THE FINAL MOVE: JAXTON WILLIS.”
His eyes blur with tears. He flips it.
CARD: “THE STORYTELLER.” A blank silhouette stares back.
Beneath it, scorched into the table: “START A STORY. REVEAL THE WOUND. PAY THE COST. ONLY THEN CAN YOU END IT.”
JAXTON (choked)
It’s not a game. It’s a possession.
83.
A voice coils through the void, too many tones in one.
PARASITIC VOICE (O.S.) One must finish the story. One must
remain. Choose. Surrender... or rewrite.
A second card forms beside it. Its back already smoking: “THE SACRIFICE.”
JAXTON (soft)
I brought them here. (beat)
And I’ll bring them home. He lays his hand flat on the table.
The card IGNITES. A fire without heat, light without mercy. The table pulses red. The room begins to UNRAVEL.
JAXTON (CONT’D) Then here’s the truth—
Stories don’t end because they finish.
(beat)
They end when we finally admit they were real. That they mattered.
The table SHUDDERS. Cards curl to ash. Dice combust to dust.
JAXTON (CONT’D) That’s the cost, isn’t it?
Truth.
The final card flips one last time. A CRACK tears the room in two. The FEEDER SCREAMS somewhere beyond.
WHITE LIGHT CONSUMES JAXTON.

event_title: Jaxton returns; states final move
event_text_verbatim:
INT. PARLOR – REALITY – MOMENTS LATER
The MAP RIPS in half. Dice EXPLODE. Wesley’s tablet glitches and DIES. The air drops twenty degrees.
Lucy, Maverick, and Lucas stare at the ruin. Silent. All hope lost.
Suddenly-
JAXTON STEPS OUT OF THE AIR.
Pale. Thinner. Eyes glowing faintly. But ALIVE.
84.
LUCY (soft)
Jax...?
JAXTON
There’s still one move left.
The TIMEPIECE TICKS. 9:13. Unstoppable.
JAXTON (CONT’D) Someone stays. Or no one leaves.
A silence so heavy it could break bone.
MAVERICK
I’ll do it. I made the first roll.
LUCAS
No. It’s not after you.
LUCY Stop.
She steps forward, trembling.
LUCY (CONT’D)
It let me in first. Chose me before
any of this.
JAXTON
No, Lucy. You weren’t chosen to
die. (beat)
You were chosen to remember. She shakes her head, devastated.
LUCY
I don’t want it.
JAXTON That’s why it’s yours.
(beat)
You weren’t meant to survive. You were meant to carry it.
He moves to the center of the parlor. The table REBUILDS itself from ash. Cards rise again, dripping ink.
JAXTON (CONT’D)

event_title: Jaxton says goodbye and surrenders
event_text_verbatim:
LUCY
(tears streaming)
Jax, please don’t go...
Jaxton turns to each of them, broken but resolute.
He takes HOLLY’S empty chair in his hand, stroking it once. His voice cracks.
JAXTON
(to her absence)
You were the light I never deserved.
He pulls MAVERICK into a rough embrace.
JAXTON (CONT’D)
You were the brother I wanted to
fight with forever. Keep laughing, Mav. Even when it hurts. Especially then.
Maverick crumbles.
He grips LUCAS by the shoulders, locking eyes.
JAXTON (CONT’D)
You saw me when no one else did.
Don’t carry my silence, Lucas. Speak louder than I ever could.
Lucas BREAKS, sobbing, clinging to him.
Finally, Jaxton looks at LUCY. Everything in him is breaking.
JAXTON (CONT’D) (to Lucy)
You gave me a world when ours ended. You kept me alive when Mom and Dad couldn’t. I love you, Luce. Always.
LUCY
Please... don’t make me lose you
again.
He kisses her forehead. Pulls back. Tears streaming down both their faces.
JAXTON
My name is Jaxton Willis.
I started the story. (beat)
And I’m ending it.
86.
He turns to the air. His voice steady now, but soft—like prayer.
JAXTON (CONT’D) I surrender.
I rewrite. I stay.
The FEEDER SHRIEKS.
Jaxton is SWALLOWED by the light. Gone. The LODGE goes SI

event_title: Survivors leave at dawn
event_text_verbatim:
INT/EXT. PARLOR – REALITY - DAWN
The front door CREAKS open. Lucy, Maverick, and Lucas step out into faint morning light. Their faces empty. Destroyed.
Behind them, the lodge is still. Dead.
A BLACK CROW perches on the gatepost. Watches. Then FLIES.
INT. PARLOR – CONTINUOUS
Empty. Ash and ruin.
A single MINIATURE tips over, Jaxton’s NEON-BEARDED GOBLIN

event_title: Feeder speaks beneath the house
event_text_verbatim:
INT. CRAWLSPACE – BENEATH THE HOUSE – SAME TIME
Dark. A slow PULSING HUM. Like a heart too old to stop.
Blackened SHADOW ROOTS twitch in the soil. In their center: THE PARASITIC DEMON. THE FEEDER.
Its form is shifting, face always changing, eyes empty yet endless.
It PULSES in shades of BLACK, speaking in vibration.
PARASITIC DEMON (V.O.) Another story told. Another door
opened. (beat)
Not yet free. But closer.
A root finds a crack. Reaches upward. Toward the palor.

event_title: Livestream continues; chat threatens
event_text_verbatim:
INT. PARLOR – REALITY - LATER
The tablet screen FLICKERS. STILL STREAMING. Text overlay: “LIVE • 1 viewer.”
CHAT: USER_0001 has entered the room.
A second line—glitched:
CHAT: Who’s next?
FAINT AUDIO: CHILD’S LAUGHTER.
A darkness seeping into the STREAM.

event_title: Weeks later: Lucy grieves and investigates
event_text_verbatim:
INT. LUCY’S APARTMENT – NIGHT – WEEKS LATER
Lucy sits in silence. Her apartment dark. Funeral programs scattered on the table. Photos of Jaxton, Holly, Wesley, Kelly, Billy.
Candles half-melted. A framed photo: Jaxton at ten, bone D20 in hand. Lucy behind him.
LUCY (V.O.)
After our parents died, it was just
me and Jax. I promised I’d take care of him.
(beat)
And I broke that promise.
She turns a notebook filled with SPIRALS.
Questions scrawled in frantic hand: “Who made the game?”, “Why me?”, “What’s the cost?”
LUCY (V.O.)
I can’t sleep. The spiral, it’s
everywhere. It followed me out of the house.
She opens a drawer. Pulls out the CRACKED TIMEPIECE. Still ticking.
LUCY (V.O.)
Sometimes I hear him. Puck. Endings
cost what truth won’t spare.
A whisper in the corners. The faint scrape of dice.
FADE TO BLACK.
She flips to a page in her journal. Names. Dates. The spiral over and over.
LUCY (V.O.)
I thought it ended in that house.
But stories like this... they don’t end. They echo.
(beat)
And I’m going to find out how.
